title: Course Capstone: How All the Pieces Fit Together
concept: Building a real-world machine learning system means connecting every piece of your journey — data prep, math, modeling, evaluation, and deployment — into a single pipeline.

exercises.prompt: Recap: What is a hybrid recommendation system?

options.text: A system that combines content-based and collaborative filtering to make better recommendations
A system that only works on hybrid/electric car data
A model that switches between regression and classification randomly

exercises.explain: Exactly! Hybrid systems merge content-based and collaborative filtering to get the benefits of both and bypass cold start issues.

cards.title: You have built the entire map
cards.body: Congratulations! You started on Day 1 wondering what AI and ML even meant. Today, on Day 180, you have traveled through the entire landscape of classical Machine Learning. You aren't just looking at individual trees anymore — you can see the entire forest.

cards.title: The machine learning pipeline
cards.body: A real-world ML system is a pipeline: 1. Raw Data is cleaned and features are engineered, 2. Math models (vectors/calculus) find the patterns, 3. The model is trained (via optimization like gradient descent), 4. The model is evaluated (via metrics like RMSE, Precision/Recall, or Cross-Validation), and 5. The model makes predictions or recommendations.

cards.title: An assembly line
cards.body: Think of a machine learning system like a car factory assembly line. If the raw steel is dirty (messy data), the car will rust. If the parts aren't measured (feature scaling), they won't fit. If the engine is never tested (model evaluation), it might fail on the road. Every single step must connect perfectly for the final car to run.

cards.title: Revisiting our movie recommender
cards.body: Now you know what actually runs behind a movie app: data cleaning removes broken logs; one-hot encoding parses movie categories; feature selection filters useless tags; vectors measure profile similarity; and collaborative filtering finds your taste twins. The magic is just a pipeline of simple, logical steps!

cards.title: ML isn't a single magical algorithm
cards.body: It's a common myth that building AI means finding the one 'perfect' algorithm that solves everything. In reality, the best models are built on clean data, careful feature engineering, and rigorous evaluation. The pipeline matters far more than any single modeling shortcut.

cards.title: Your Machine Learning Journey
cards.body: The core phases you have mastered:

exercises.prompt: When building a machine learning system, which step should you perform first?

options.text: Collect and prepare your data (cleaning, feature engineering)
Evaluate the model using test data

exercises.explain: Correct! Data collection and preparation are always the very first steps in the machine learning pipeline.

exercises.prompt: True or false: A highly sophisticated machine learning model will perform perfectly even if you feed it completely uncleaned, messy, and irrelevant features.
exercises.explain: False! Garbage in, garbage out — model quality is always limited by the quality of the features and data you supply.

exercises.sentence: A machine learning ___ connects data preparation, training, and evaluation into a single automated workflow.
exercises.explain: A pipeline automates the sequential steps of preparing data, training models, and serving predictions.

exercises.prompt: Which component is NOT a part of the machine learning pipeline?

options.text: Designing a customized visual user interface dashboard
Feature engineering to encode categorical columns

exercises.explain: Correct! Designing a dashboard user interface is a frontend development task, not a stage in the core machine learning pipeline (which focuses on data prep, model training, and evaluation).

exercises.prompt: You have completed Day 180 and built a complete foundation in classical Machine Learning! What is the single most important lesson to take away as you move forward to build real-world systems?

options.text: Successful machine learning is about the whole pipeline — clean data, smart features, careful math, and honest evaluation working together
Always pick the most complex model possible to guarantee success
Never evaluate your model, since training data performance is all that matters
Only mathematical formulas make a model learn, data preparation is irrelevant

exercises.explain: Absolutely correct! A successful machine learning system is the sum of its parts. By mastering the full pipeline from raw data to robust evaluation, you have built a powerful, real-world skillset. Congratulations on finishing your 180-day journey of AI learning! You have officially graduated!
